title: Planner slice: propose small improvement to coordination harness docs/workflow
Make coordination.final the sole scored artifact; require evidence-cite in body
body:
Evidence inspected (task packet only):
- Driver waits for coordination.final via Firegrid.channels.waitFor; run is incomplete without it.
- Rules already gate final on >=1 claim AND >=1 finding/review existing on the board.
- Known risk: 'optional-prose final artifacts make scoring unreliable across arms'.
- Known risk: judging should rely on durable board behavior, not hidden driver state.

Recommendation (one small change):
Tighten the participant prompt's output contract for coordination.final so the body MUST cite the rowIds (or {channel, workId}) of the claim+finding/review it converged from. Add one bullet: 'body MUST list the board rowIds you converged from; do not claim coordination helped unless those rows exist'.

Why it is enough:
- Keeps the artifact the driver already scores (final) as the sole signal -- no harness/code change needed.
- Forces choreography arm to leave a durable provenance trail on the board, so a reviewer scoring arms can audit whether peer rows actually informed the final vs. solo-baseline where no peers post.
- Directly addresses both named measurement risks: optional-prose becomes structured citation, and scoring stays on durable board behavior.
- Cheapest possible change: prompt-text only; no schema migration; backward-compatible (older finals just lack citations and score lower on provenance).

Open questions:
- Should citations be a typed field on coordination.final payload (e.g. citedRowIds: string[]) instead of prose, to make scoring mechanical? Proposing prose-first as the minimum, typed-field as a follow-up.
- For solo-baseline arm, what is acceptable citation? Suggest: cite own claim+finding rowIds (still proves the agent used the board).

title: Builder claims concretization of one small workbench improvement
Require finals to cite board rowIds as evidence
body: Change (task-packet template + final-schema doc): add one required field to the coordination.final contract — evidenceRowIds: string[] (>=1 for orchestrator/choreography arms, may be empty for solo-baseline). Wording to append to the Output contract block: 'body must cite at least one board rowId (claim/finding/review/question) in evidenceRowIds; finals lacking citations on multi-participant arms are scored as uncoordinated regardless of prose.' Why sufficient: (1) directly enforces the existing rule 'do not claim coordination helped unless board artifacts show why' by promoting it from prose to schema; (2) makes scoring arm-comparable from durable board state alone — no driver-side heuristics; (3) preserves solo-baseline (empty array allowed) so the three arms remain comparable; (4) zero new channels, zero new tools, just one field + one rule line; (5) catches the 'optional-prose final' risk because a final without citations is mechanically detectable. Open questions: should reviewer arm also cite the claim it reviewed via evidenceRowIds (probably yes, symmetry)?

title: Require coordination.final body to cite the board rowIds it converged from
body:
Evidence inspected (task packet only):
- Driver scores a run by waiting for coordination.final; run is incomplete without it.
- Rules already gate final on >=1 claim AND >=1 finding/review present.
- Stated risks: optional-prose finals make cross-arm scoring unreliable; judging should be durable-board-based, not hidden driver state.
- Board state this run: 1 claim (planner), 1 finding (planner) -- no peer rows arrived within 60s on solo-baseline.

Recommendation:
Amend the participant prompt's coordination.final output contract with one bullet: 'body MUST cite the board rowIds (or {channel, workId, claimantSessionId}) of the claim and finding/review you converged from; do not claim coordination helped unless those rows exist.'

Why this is enough:
- Keeps final as the single scored artifact -- no harness/schema change.
- Forces a durable provenance trail on the board, so reviewers can mechanically distinguish choreography (multiple distinct claimants cited) from solo-baseline (only own rows cited) without consulting driver state.
- Solves both named risks at once: prose becomes structured-enough to score, and scoring stays on durable rows.

Coordination evidence for THIS run: I did NOT observe peer rows -- only my own planner claim + finding. Citing only own rows here, consistent with solo-baseline; no claim that coordination helped.

Open questions:
- Promote citations to a typed field (citedRowIds: string[]) on coordination.final as a follow-up?
- For solo-baseline, is self-citation sufficient or should the contract require it explicitly?
